description: Physiotherapy and rehabilitation vocabulary. Covers functional independence assessment (FIM — the internationally standardised ADL instrument for inpatient rehabilitation), upper and lower limb sub-scores, hand mobility measurement, and pulmonary function testing (spirometry). These ConvSets map to the WHO ICF framework and carry LOINC mappings for interoperability.

notes.168260: Functional Independence Measure — total (ConvSet) — composite FIM score construct covering all 18 ADL domains: self-care, sphincter control, transfer, locomotion, communication, and social cognition (~20 concepts)
notes.168309: FIM lower limb construct (ConvSet) — lower extremity FIM sub-scores: transfers (bed/chair/wheelchair, toilet, tub/shower), locomotion (walk/wheelchair, stairs) (~10 concepts)
notes.168306: FIM upper limb construct (ConvSet) — upper extremity FIM sub-scores: self-care items (eating, grooming, bathing, dressing upper/lower body, toileting) (~8 concepts)
notes.168073: Spirometry results set (ConvSet) — pulmonary function test values: FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC ratio, PEFR, and bronchodilator response; carries LOINC mappings (~10 concepts)
notes.168278: Hand analytic mobility questions (ConvSet) — hand and grip strength measurement items used in hand therapy and orthopaedic rehabilitation assessment (~8 concepts)
